questions.explanation: WPA2 mandates AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) with CCMP for encryption and message integrity. CCMP replaced TKIP (RC4-based, used in WPA). WPA2 with AES/CCMP is the current enterprise security standard.

questions.question: A host cannot communicate with any device on a different VLAN. The host's port is correctly in VLAN 10. What should be checked next?
options: Whether inter-VLAN routing is configured (Layer 3 switch SVIs or router-on-a-stick) and the trunk link is up
Whether the switch has the latest firmware
questions.explanation: Hosts in different VLANs require a Layer 3 device for routing. Check: (1) Is inter-VLAN routing configured? (2) Is the trunk operational? (3) Are SVIs up/up? (4) Do routing table entries exist for both VLANs?

questions.question: What is STP Loop Guard and what problem does it solve?
options: Protects against a port transitioning to forwarding when BPDUs stop being received (e.g., due to a unidirectional link failure) — prevents accidental loops
questions.explanation: Loop Guard puts a port into 'loop-inconsistent' state when BPDUs stop arriving (instead of transitioning to forwarding). Without Loop Guard, a non-designated port that stops seeing BPDUs would incorrectly move to forwarding.

questions.question: What is a private VLAN (PVLAN)?
options: A VLAN not advertised by VTP
A VLAN feature that creates sub-VLANs: isolated (no intra-VLAN communication) and community (communication only within group) — used for ISP/hosting environments
questions.explanation: PVLANs create isolation within a VLAN. Primary VLAN contains secondary VLANs: Isolated (ports cannot communicate with each other), Community (ports can communicate within group). Promiscuous port can reach all.

questions.question: What does the STP topology change notification (TCN) trigger?
options: Switches reduce their MAC address table aging timer to flush stale entries faster after a topology change
questions.explanation: When STP detects a topology change (port moves to/from forwarding), it sends TCN BPDUs toward the root. The root floods TC BPDUs causing all bridges to reduce MAC aging to forward_delay (15s) — flushing stale MAC entries.

questions.question: What are the three VTP modes and what does each do?
questions.explanation: VTP modes: Server (creates/modifies/deletes VLANs, syncs to domain), Client (receives VLAN info, cannot create VLANs), Transparent (doesn't participate in VTP but forwards VTP advertisements). VTP Off mode also exists in v3.

questions.question: What is BSS coloring in 802.11ax (Wi-Fi 6)?
options: A mechanism that colors (labels) BSS transmissions so devices can distinguish overlapping BSS traffic — allowing spatial reuse instead of deferring
questions.explanation: BSS coloring assigns a color (number) to each BSS. Devices hearing a different-colored BSS transmission can determine it's not their BSS and transmit simultaneously (spatial reuse), significantly improving spectral efficiency in dense deployments.

questions.question: A switch port shows 'err-disabled'. What are common causes?
options: Port security violation, BPDU Guard triggered, unidirectional link, or LoopGuard inconsistency — the port is disabled by IOS until manually re-enabled
The connected device is powered off
questions.explanation: Err-disabled causes: port security violation, BPDU Guard BPDU received, storm control, LoopGuard inconsistency, UDLD unidirectional link. Fix: `shutdown` then `no shutdown` on the port (after resolving the root cause).

questions.question: Two switches are connected by two links. One link is forwarding; the other is blocking. What would happen if BPDU Guard was mistakenly enabled on the blocking port?
options: Nothing — BPDU Guard ignores internal BPDUs
The port would err-disable when it receives BPDUs from the other switch — breaking redundancy
The forwarding port would also block
questions.explanation: BPDU Guard disables a port when ANY BPDU is received. On an inter-switch link, BPDUs are always present. Applying BPDU Guard to trunk/uplink ports is a misconfiguration — it's intended only for access ports facing end devices.

questions.question: How does Rapid PVST+ (802.1w) achieve faster convergence than 802.1D?
options: Uses proposal/agreement handshake mechanism between switches to immediately transition designated ports to forwarding without waiting for timers
questions.explanation: Rapid STP uses a Proposal/Agreement handshake: a switch proposes its port as designated, the downstream switch agrees (after setting all non-edge ports to discarding), and the link immediately transitions to forwarding — no timer wait.

questions.question: What load-balancing method does EtherChannel use by default on Cisco switches?
options: Source MAC address hashing (src-mac) — all traffic from the same source MAC uses the same physical link
questions.explanation: Default EtherChannel load balancing uses source MAC hashing. Other options: dst-mac, src-dst-mac, src-ip, dst-ip, src-dst-ip. Change with `port-channel load-balance <method>`. Hashing ensures a single flow always uses the same link.

questions.question: What command verifies which load-balancing algorithm an EtherChannel is using?
questions.explanation: `show etherchannel load-balance` displays the configured load-balancing method (e.g., Source MAC address). `show etherchannel detail` shows member interface status, age, and flags.

questions.question: Why is VLAN 1 considered a security concern on Cisco switches?
options: VLAN 1 is the default for all ports and carries native management traffic (STP, CDP, DTP, VTP) — mixing user traffic in VLAN 1 creates security risk
questions.explanation: Best practice: change the native VLAN to an unused VLAN, put user traffic in separate VLANs, and avoid VLAN 1 for production traffic. VLAN 1 carries untagged management frames and is a common attack target.

questions.question: What does a wireless site survey accomplish?
options: Physically assesses an area to determine optimal AP placement, channel assignments, and identify interference sources — ensuring adequate coverage before deployment
questions.explanation: A site survey maps RF propagation, locates interference (microwaves, Bluetooth, neighboring APs), identifies coverage holes, and validates AP placement for required signal strength before permanent installation.

questions.question: What is a VLAN trunk allowed VLAN list and what is its default?
options: All VLANs (1-4094) by default — use `switchport trunk allowed vlan` to restrict
questions.explanation: By default, all VLANs (1-4094) are allowed on an 802.1Q trunk. Restrict with: `switchport trunk allowed vlan <list>` (replaces list) or `add <vlan>` (adds to list) or `remove <vlan>` (removes from list) or `except <vlan>`.

questions.question: What 802.11 physical layer technique uses multiple antennas to improve throughput via spatial multiplexing?
questions.explanation: MIMO uses multiple transmit and receive antennas to send multiple data streams simultaneously on the same channel (spatial multiplexing). 802.11n introduced MIMO; 802.11ac uses MU-MIMO (Multiple-User MIMO) for concurrent multi-client streams.

questions.question: What is a switch's aging timer for MAC address table entries?
options: 300 seconds (5 minutes) by default — entries not seen within this period are removed to prevent stale MACs
questions.explanation: The default MAC address table aging time on Cisco switches is 300 seconds (5 minutes). Inactivity for that period causes the entry to be removed. During topology changes, STP may temporarily reduce this to 15 seconds (forward_delay).

questions.question: What information does CDP (Cisco Discovery Protocol) exchange between neighbors?
options: Device ID, IP address, platform, capabilities (Router/Switch), IOS version, and interface information — Layer 2 discovery, no IP routing required
questions.explanation: CDP (Layer 2, multicast) advertises: Device ID (hostname), IP address, platform (hardware model), capabilities (R=Router, S=Switch, T=Transparent), IOS version, and local/remote interface. Useful for network discovery and documentation.

questions.question: Which command assigns VLAN 10 to a Cisco switch access port?
questions.explanation: 'switchport access vlan 10' in interface config mode places the port in VLAN 10 as an access port.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 14; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 8

questions.question: What is the default native VLAN on a Cisco trunk port?
questions.explanation: VLAN 1 is the default native VLAN on Cisco IOS trunk ports. Native VLAN frames are sent untagged across the trunk.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 17; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 8

questions.question: Which STP port state forwards data frames AND learns MAC addresses?
questions.explanation: Only the Forwarding state both learns MACs and forwards frames. The Learning state learns MACs but does not forward. Blocking and Listening do neither.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 20; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 9

questions.question: Which RSTP port role replaces the STP Blocking port and provides an alternate path to the root bridge?
questions.explanation: RSTP introduces the Alternate port role — it provides a backup path to the root bridge and immediately transitions to Forwarding if the root port fails (no STP convergence delay).
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 21; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 9

questions.question: What does the 'spanning-tree portfast' command do when applied to an access port?
options: Disables STP on the port entirely
Allows the port to skip the Listening and Learning states
Sets the port as the STP root
questions.explanation: PortFast skips Listening and Learning, putting the port directly into Forwarding. It should only be used on edge ports (connected to end hosts), never on trunk/uplink ports.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 20; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 9

questions.question: Which EtherChannel negotiation protocol is Cisco proprietary?
questions.explanation: PAgP (Port Aggregation Protocol) is Cisco proprietary. LACP (Link Aggregation Control Protocol) is the IEEE 802.3ad open standard equivalent.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 23; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 10

questions.question: An EtherChannel is formed using LACP. Which two port modes will form a channel? (Choose one pair)
questions.explanation: LACP Active–Active will form a channel. Active–Passive also works. Passive–Passive will not form a channel (neither side initiates). 'On' mode uses no negotiation protocol and won't interoperate with LACP modes.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 23; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 10

questions.question: Port security is configured with 'switchport port-security maximum 2' and 'switchport port-security violation restrict'. What happens when a third MAC is seen?
options: The frame is dropped and a syslog/SNMP notification is generated, but the port stays up
The new MAC replaces the oldest secure MAC
All traffic on the port is blocked
questions.explanation: 'restrict' mode drops traffic from violating MACs and increments the violation counter (generating a syslog/SNMP trap) but keeps the port up. 'shutdown' (default) err-disables the port; 'protect' drops frames silently.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 19; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 8

questions.question: Which command displays the MAC address table on a Cisco switch?
questions.explanation: 'show mac-address-table' displays the switch's Layer 2 forwarding table, including dynamic and static MAC entries with their associated VLANs and ports.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 8; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 7

questions.question: Which IEEE standard defines 802.1Q VLAN tagging?
questions.explanation: IEEE 802.1Q defines the standard for VLAN tagging (inserting a 4-byte tag into Ethernet frames). 802.1D is original STP; 802.1w is RSTP; 802.1s is MSTP.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 17; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 8

questions.question: What is the purpose of BPDU Guard?
options: Prevents a port from becoming root bridge
Err-disables a PortFast port if a BPDU is received
Speeds up STP convergence on uplink ports
questions.explanation: BPDU Guard err-disables a PortFast-enabled port the moment it receives a BPDU — protecting against unauthorized switches being connected to edge ports.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 20; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 9

questions.question: Which command enables CDP globally on a Cisco device?
questions.explanation: 'cdp run' (global config) enables CDP globally. 'cdp enable' is the interface-level command. CDP is enabled by default on Cisco devices.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 36; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 26

questions.question: How many bytes does an 802.1Q VLAN tag add to an Ethernet frame?
questions.explanation: The 802.1Q tag is 4 bytes: 2 bytes for the Tag Protocol Identifier (0x8100) and 2 bytes containing the Priority Code Point, DEI bit, and 12-bit VLAN ID.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 17; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 8

questions.question: Switch A has priority 32768 and MAC 0011.2233.4455. Switch B has priority 28672 and MAC 00AA.BBCC.DDEE. Which switch is elected root bridge?
questions.explanation: Root bridge election: lowest Bridge ID wins. Bridge ID = priority (2 bytes) + MAC (6 bytes). Priority is compared first: 28672 < 32768, so Switch B wins regardless of MAC.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 20; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 9

questions.question: Which trunk encapsulation type is required before setting a port to trunk mode on a Layer 3-capable switch?
questions.explanation: On Layer 3 switches supporting both ISL and 802.1Q, you must first run 'switchport trunk encapsulation dot1q' before 'switchport mode trunk'. Layer 2-only switches with only 802.1Q support don't require this step.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 17; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 8

questions.question: What is the STP path cost for a 1 Gbps link using the short (original) cost method?
questions.explanation: Short STP costs: 10 Mbps=100, 100 Mbps=19, 1 Gbps=4, 10 Gbps=2. The long method scales higher. 1 Gbps short cost = 4.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 20; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 9

questions.question: Which command verifies EtherChannel bundle status on a Cisco switch?
questions.explanation: 'show etherchannel summary' displays all port-channel bundles, their protocol (LACP/PAgP/Static), and member port states (bundled, suspended, etc.).
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 23; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 10

questions.question: VLAN 1 is Cisco's default VLAN and cannot be deleted from the VLAN database.
questions.explanation: VLAN 1 is the default VLAN and is also the default native VLAN on trunks. Cisco does not allow VLAN 1 to be deleted, though you can remove it from allowed VLANs on a trunk.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 14; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 8

questions.question: PortFast should be enabled on ports connected to other switches.
questions.explanation: PortFast should ONLY be enabled on access ports connected to end hosts (PCs, servers). Enabling PortFast on a port connected to another switch can cause bridging loops since BPDUs won't be expected — BPDU Guard should also be enabled.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 20; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 9

questions.question: EtherChannel load balances traffic by sending each frame of a single TCP flow over a different physical link.
questions.explanation: EtherChannel load balances per-flow (not per-frame) by hashing source/destination MAC, IP, or port to select a physical link. All frames of a single flow use the same link, maintaining packet order within the flow.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 23; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 10

questions.question: LLDP is an IEEE open standard equivalent of Cisco's CDP.
questions.explanation: LLDP (Link Layer Discovery Protocol — IEEE 802.1AB) is the vendor-neutral equivalent of Cisco's CDP. Both discover directly connected neighbors and share information like device type, capabilities, and port IDs.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 36; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 26

questions.question: Order the STP port states from initial link-up to full forwarding (classic 802.1D):
items: Forwarding
Blocking
Listening
Learning
questions.explanation: STP 802.1D port state progression: Blocking (20s) → Listening (15s) → Learning (15s) → Forwarding. Total convergence ~50 seconds. RSTP eliminates most of these delays.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 20; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 9

questions.question: Order the steps to configure a VLAN and assign it to an access port on a Cisco switch:
items: interface GigabitEthernet0/1
vlan database / configure terminal
switchport mode access
vlan 10 (create VLAN)
switchport access vlan 10
questions.explanation: 1) Enter config mode, 2) Create VLAN 10, 3) Enter interface config, 4) Set port as access, 5) Assign VLAN 10.
questions.source_ref: Jeremy's IT Lab Day 14; CCNA 200-301 OCG Ch. 8

questions.question: What is a basic security measure used in some wireless networks?
questions.explanation: A simple security measure that is sometimes used in combination with stronger measures is to prevent the broadcast of the SSID; this is known as network cloaking.

questions.question: What wireless standard introduced in 2013 permits a maximum speed of 6.93 Gbps?
questions.explanation: 802.11ac, which was introduced in 2013, shattered previous maximum throughput limits. This technology uses the 5 GHz band and allows a maximum speed of 6.93 Gbps. Additional Resource Additional Resource WiFi Standards (and how they work) https://youtu.be/q64AZjPfa0Y

questions.question: What is a popular single-pane-of-glass approach to managing a Cisco WLC and other key Cisco components of the network infrastructure?
questions.explanation: Cisco DNA Center is a single-pane-of-glass solution for the manage- ment of Cisco WLCs and other Cisco devices.

questions.question: What is the total throughput possible with a Cisco 9800 series WLC?
questions.explanation: 9800 series devices offer total throughput of 5 Gbps.

questions.question: Which of the following are supported QoS settings for a WLAN built by a WLC?
questions.explanation: QoS settings are possible through profiles. You can configure mobility support, marking and policing, and even prebuilt settings based on traffic classes. Additional Resource Additional Resource Fundamentals of Wireless Controllers https://youtu.be/ZFTgI-2gWsI

questions.question: If you are running WPA3 in enterprise mode, what is the required version of AES?
questions.explanation: WPA3 provides AES-192 as an option in personal mode and as a requirement in enterprise mode. Additional Resources WiFi (Wireless) Password Security - WEP, WPA, WPA2, WPS Explained https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WZaIfyvERcA Next-Gen Wi-Fi Security - WPA3 Explained https://youtu.be/aPoe4WtX2mU

questions.question: What is the IPv4 address range 224.0.0.0 to 239.255.255.255 used for?
options: To send a packet to all systems
To send a packet to a group of systems
To send a packet to a single specific system
To send multiple packets to only a single specific system
questions.explanation: 224.0.0.0 to 239.255.255.255 is the IPv4 multicast address range. This range permits the sending of a single packet to a group of machines that “subscribe” to the traffic. This is unlike a broadcast, where a single packet is sent to all systems. In IPv6, broadcast traffic is eliminated in favor of multicast.

questions.question: Which RF band is used with 802.11ac?
questions.explanation: 802.11ac uses the 5 GHz band.

questions.question: How is a playbook expressed in Ansible?
questions.explanation: Ansible playbooks are expressed in the YAML format, which was chosen, in part, for its readability. For more information, see https://docs.ansible.com/ansible/latest/user_guide/playbooks_intro.html.

questions.question: What wireless security standard replaces PSK with SAE?
questions.explanation: WPA3 introduces many improvements over WPA2, including the replacement of the PSK authentication method.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an HTTP method used with REST APIs?
questions.explanation: SEND is not an HTTP method.

questions.question: Which of the following addresses is a private-use-only address?
Examine the access list shown here. What is the issue with this access list?
options: The access list cannot end with a deny statement.
The access list permits the traffic before it denies any traffic.
The access list is not named, and naming is required.
Standard access lists do not support logging.
questions.explanation: The order of access list statements is very important; they are processed from top to bottom. Here, the permit statement that begins the list permits all traffic before the deny statements are processed. On many IOS routers, the subsequent “deny” access control entries wouldn’t even be allowed and would generate a message indicating a conflict due to the permit any entry already being in place.

questions.question: What information for a wireless network can you choose not to broadcast?
questions.explanation: You can choose not to broadcast the name of the network (that is, the SSID). Note that not broadcasting the SSID does not add to the overall security of the implementation.

questions.question: What port security approach is considered a mix of dynamic and static configuration?
questions.explanation: Port security sticky learning means the port will initially dynamically learn the MAC addresses and add them to the running configuration. After this, this updated running config can be saved (using the command copy running-config startup-config) in the startup configuration as a static MAC address assignment for port security for when the switch reboots.

questions.question: What networking device provides instructions to APs?
questions.explanation: A wireless LAN controller is a device in a modern network that controls and manages access points.

questions.question: Which of the following is essentially a standards-based version of HSRP?
questions.explanation: VRRP is very similar to HSRP. This FHRP is standards based.

questions.question: What vty line command causes the local username database to be checked for remote access?
questions.explanation: The login local command ensures the use of the local user account’s database on the device when connecting to the vty lines.

questions.question: What will be the correct command on a Cisco router to set the IP address of an interface assuming you are at the "router(config-if)#" prompt?
questions.explanation: [CxNx] What will be the correct command on a Cisco router to set the IP address of an interface assuming you are at the "router(config-if)#" prompt? a) ip address 1.1.1.1 255.255.255.0 b) ip address 1.1.1.1 c) ip 1.1.1.1 d) ip 1.1.1.1 255.255.255.0 e) none of the above Answer a) ip address 1.1.1.1 255.255.255.0 If you are not in the configuration mode, the ip address can be set by using the setup mode. Configuring interface Ethernet0/0: Is this i

questions.explanation: [CxNx] Which commands will display the IP addresses of all interfaces on a Cisco Router? (Choose all that apply) a) show ip route b) show ip address c) show ip interface d) show interface e) display ip addresses Answer c) show ip interface d) show interface Examples of each of these commands are shown below: Router_A#sh int e0/0 Ethernet0/0 is up, line protocol is up Hardware is AmdP2, address is 0010.7b15.bd41 (bia 0010.7b15.bd41) Internet addre

questions.question: Which of the following layers defines data representation?
questions.explanation: [OxNx] Which of the following layers defines data representation? a) Application Layer 7 b) Presentation Layer 6 c) Session Layer 5 d) Transport Layer 4 e) Network Layer 3 f) Data Link Layer 2 g) Physical Layer 1 Answer b) Presentation Layer 6 Questions 1-19 | 20-39 | 40-59 | 60-71 | 72-78 Top of Page

questions.question: Which of the following layers sends and receives binary information?
Which of the following layers provides end to end connections?
questions.explanation: [OxNx] Which of the following layers provides end to end connections? a) Application Layer 7 b) Presentation Layer 6 c) Session Layer 5 d) Transport Layer 4 e) Network Layer 3 f) Data Link Layer 2 g) Physical Layer 1 Answer d) Transport Layer 4

questions.question: Which of the following layers controls access to the media?
Raw data is converted to segments at which layer?
questions.explanation: [OxNx] Raw data is converted to segments at which layer? a) Application Layer 7 b) Presentation Layer 6 c) Session Layer 5 d) Transport Layer 4 e) Network Layer 3 f) Data Link Layer 2 g) Physical Layer 1 Answer d) Transport Layer 4

questions.question: Which one of the following layers "best" describes the ability of the upper layers to gain independence over LAN media access?
On an IP over Token Ring network, which answer best describes the role of ARP?
options: ARP is used to map an IP address
ARP is used to map Ring numbers to IP addresses.
ARP is used to map Ring numbers to MAC addresses of the hosts on that ring.
ARP is used to map a MAC address to an IP address.
questions.explanation: [RINx] On an IP over Token Ring network, which answer best describes the role of ARP? (Choose one.) a) ARP is used to map an IP address to a MAC address. b) ARP is used to map Ring numbers to IP addresses. c) ARP is used to map Ring numbers to MAC addresses of the hosts on that ring. d) ARP is used to map a MAC address to an IP address. e) ARP is not used on Token Ring networks. Answer a) ARP is used to map an IP address to a MAC address. Address

questions.question: Which of the following are true of an Appletalk address?
options: The address has a network and a host part.
The node part is usually the MAC address of the host.

Answer a) the total address length is 24 bits b) the address has a network and a host part The following is a portion of a router configuration: interface Serial1 no ip address appletalk ca

options: Multiple data segments to be sent
Multiple frame types to be sent over the same media type.
Multiple data segments to be sent before the
Separate data streams from different hosts to a common host.

questions.question: A lightweight access point in which one of the following architectures participate?
questions.explanation: Split-MAC is the correct answer. On a lightweight AP, the MAC function is divided between the AP hardware and the WLC. The LAP-WLC division of labor is known as split-MAC architecture. The Split MAC architecture divides the implementation of the MAC functions between the AP and the controller. The 802.11 AP at its simplest level is the 802.11 radio MAC layer providing bridging to a wired network for the WLAN client as- sociated to the AP Basic Se

questions.question: Which of the following wireless security tools is used to protect the integrity of data in a wireless frame?
questions.explanation: MIC is the correct answer. A message integrity check (MIC) is a security tool that can protect against data tampering. You can think of a MIC as a way for the sender to add a secret 275 stamp inside the encrypted data frame. The stamp is based on the contents of the data bits to be transmitted. Once the recipient decrypts the frame, it can compare the se- cret stamp to its own idea of what the stamp should be, based on the data bits that were rec

questions.question: Which of the following bridges can be used to provide wireless connectivity to a non-wireless device?
questions.explanation: Workgroup bridge is the correct answer. A workgroup bridge acts as a wireless client, but bridges traf- fic to and from a wired device connected to it. In workgroup bridge mode, the device associates to another access point as a client and provides a network connection for the equipment connected to its Ethernet port. For example, if you need to provide wireless connectivity for a group of network printers, you can connect the printers to a hub o

questions.question: Which controller interface type maps a WLAN to a VLAN?
questions.explanation: Dynamic interface is the correct answer. Cisco controllers support the following interface types: 1. Dynamic interface: Used to connect a VLAN to a WLAN. 2. Management interface: Used for normal management traf- fic, such as RADIUS user authentication, WLC-to-WLC com- munication, web-based, and SSH sessions, SNMP, Network Time Protocol (NTP), syslog, and so on. The management in- terface is also used to terminate CAPWAP tunnels between the contro

questions.question: You are creating a new WLAN with the controller GUI, which of the following parameters are necessary? (Choose two)
questions.explanation: SSID and Interface are the correct an- swers. The SSID and controller interface are the only parame- ters from the list that are necessary in order to create a new WLAN.

questions.question: When FlexConnect local switching is enabled for a WLAN, which device is responsible for switching traffic between the 802.11 and Ethernet networks?
questions.explanation: 64 characters The WPA2 PSK can be configured as 64 hexadecimal characters. Alternatively, it can be configured as 8–63 ASCII characters.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which LWAP mode is dedicated to analyzing traffic on the wired LAN?
questions.explanation: Silver The Silver QoS level is the default and is used for best-effort treatment of traffic. The other levels are: Platinum (voice), Gold (video), and Bronze (background).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: In Cisco Meraki’s cloud-based AP solution, which traffic is tunneled to the Meraki cloud?
questions.explanation: Client Band Select Client Band Select is a feature that delays responses to 2.4 GHz probe requests to encourage clients to connect to the 5 GHz band, which is usually less crowded.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which WLC deployment option uses a dedicated hardware appliance?
questions.explanation: Passthrough D: Conditional Web Redirect Passthrough and Conditional Web Redirect are both Layer-3 WLAN security settings. Passthrough allows the user to access the network after accepting certain terms and conditions or viewing a mandatory message. Conditional Web Redirect redirects the user to a specific web page under certain conditions (i.e. their password is expired).
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which of the following functions are centrally controlled in a split-MAC architecture? (select two)
questions.explanation: Dynamic interface Dynamic interfaces on a Cisco WLC are used to map WLANs to VLANs. Each dynamic interface is mapped to a VLAN on the wired network and a corresponding WLAN on the wireless network.

questions.question: Which statement about CAPWAP is true?
options: The CAPWAP control tunnel is encrypted by default.
The CAPWAP data tunnel is encrypted by default.
Both CAPWAP tunnels are encrypted by default.
questions.explanation: Wireless The Wireless tab in the Cisco WLC GUI is where you configure settings related to the LWAPs managed by the WLC.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which of the following is not a benefit of cloud-based network management?
options: Highly customizable to meet complex networking requirements
Simplified management via a web-based dashboard
questions.explanation: All DS ports When you enable link aggregation group (LAG), all DS ports are included in the LAG. However, not all DS ports need to be active; as long as there is one functioning physical port in the LAG, it will be operational.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which 802.11 security protocol was developed to overcome the vulnerabilities of WEP?
questions.explanation: HTTP D: HTTPS The graphical user interface (GUI) of a Cisco wireless LAN controller (WLC) can be accessed via HTTP or HTTPS in a web browser. Telnet and SSH are used for remote access to the command-line interface (CLI), and the console port can be used to access the CLI with a console cable.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which authentication methods are available in WEP? (select two)
questions.explanation: Distribution system port A WLC’s distribution system (DS) ports are the standard network ports that connect to the DS—the switched Ethernet LAN. User data traffic is sent from and received on these ports.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which WPA3 feature protects the PSK against brute-force attacks?
questions.explanation: Service port The service port on a Cisco WLC is used for out-of-band (OOB) management—connecting to and managing the WLC via a dedicated connection that is separate from the DS ports.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which of the following use AES counter mode encryption? (select all that apply)
questions.explanation: AP-manager interface The AP-manager interface on a Cisco WLC is used to establish CAPWAP tunnels with LWAPs. By default, the management interface acts as the AP-manager interface, but you can optionally configure a separate AP-manager interface. For this reason, option D is more correct than option C; although the management interface sometimes acts as the AP-manager interface, that is not always the case.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch18: Wireless Fund

questions.question: Which protocol is used to carry EAP messages between a supplicant and authenticator?
questions.explanation: Service-port interface The service-port interface corresponds to the physical service port and is used for OOB management. The management interface, on the other hand, is the default interface for in-band management via the DS ports.

questions.question: Which WPA3 feature ensures that a compromised PSK cannot be used to decrypt past communications?

questions.question: Which of the following uses 802.1X authentication?
questions.explanation: SBI The southbound interface (SBI) of an SDN controller is used to communicate with the managed devices. The name comes from how SDN architecture is usually illustrated, with the Application Layer at the top, the Control Layer in the middle, and the Infrastructure Layer (where the managed devices are located) at the bottom.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which of the following employs SAE?
questions.explanation: Infrastructure Layer The Infrastructure Layer in SDN architecture contains the physical and virtual network devices responsible for forwarding messages across the network. This layer is where the Data Plane’s forwarding of messages takes place.

questions.question: Which protocol does the original WPA standard employ for data encryption and integrity?

questions.question: Which protocol does the WPA3 standard employ for the most secure data encryption and integrity?
questions.explanation: SSH B: NTP Secure Shell (SSH) and Network Time Protocol (NTP) are protocols used in the management layer of network systems. SSH provides secure access to the CLI of network devices, and NTP ensures accurate time synchronization across the network.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which of the following methods can be used to connect to the GUI of a Cisco WLC? (select all that apply)
questions.explanation: Control Plane The Control Plane is responsible for the exchange of routing protocol messages, like BGP, between routers. This plane handles the logic needed to build and maintain routing tables and other tables necessary for forwarding messages.

questions.question: Which WLC port type is used to carry user data traffic?
questions.explanation: Control Plane The Control Plane in a switch is responsible for examining the source MAC addresses of incoming frames and creating MAC address table entries, which are used for making forwarding decisions. The forwarding decisions themselves are the role of the Data Plane.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which WLC port type is used for OOB management of the WLC over the network?
questions.explanation: Data Plane The Data Plane (or Forwarding Plane) in a switch is responsible for examining the destination MAC addresses of incoming frames and making forwarding or flooding decisions based on this information. Any activities related to the actual forwarding of messages are the responsibility of the Data Plane.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which WLC interface type is used to establish CAPWAP tunnels with LWAPs?
questions.explanation: Management plane B: Control plane C: Data plane In a traditional network architecture, all three planes are distributed. Each network device is managed individually, builds its own tables for forwarding traffic, and makes independent forwarding decisions.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which WLC interface type is used for OOB management of the WLC over the network?
questions.explanation: The Data Plane is distributed D: The Control Plane is centralized In software-defined networking (SDN) architecture, the Data Plane remains distributed; each network device is responsible for forwarding messages across the network. The Control Plane, on the other hand, is centralized in the SDN controller, which is responsible for providing instructions about how to forward messages to the network devices.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch19: Wireless Arch

questions.question: Which advanced WLAN feature encourages clients to associate with less-busy LWAPs?

REST APIs are stateless, meaning that each request is a separate event, independent of all past exchanges between the client and server. Each request from the client must contain all the information needed to understand and process the request. REST is based on a client-server architecture in which the client and server are independent of each other.

questions.question: Which Cisco SDN solution takes advantage of spine-leaf architecture?
questions.explanation: OAuth 2.0 OAuth 2.0 is designed to allow third-party applications to access resources on behalf of the resource owner without sharing the owner’s credentials. The resource owner grants authorization to the third-party client application, which uses the authorization grant to obtain an access token. This token allows the client to access the resource on behalf of the owner.

questions.question: Which of the following are Management Layer protocols? (select two)

The access token is typically short-lived, requiring regular renewal for continued access.
questions.source_ref: Acing the CCNA Vol2 — Ch21: Wireless Config

questions.question: Which of the following is not a benefit of network automation?
questions.explanation: OAuth 2.0 allows third-party applications to access resources without sharing the user’s credentials. OAuth 2.0 provides a secure way to delegate access to resources by issuing tokens, thereby eliminating the need to share the user’s credentials with third-party applications. OAuth 2.0 is a key component of many modern web applications. Chapter 24: Data formats

questions.question: When a frame enters a Cisco switch, what field does the switch learn from?
questions.explanation: The switch examines and learns the source MAC addresses of incoming frames.

questions.question: What is the name of the database that stores address information in a Cisco switch?
questions.explanation: The database is termed the MAC address table.

questions.question: What command allows you to view the addresses learned by a Cisco switch?
questions.explanation: The command is show mac address-table.

questions.question: Why might padding be used in an Ethernet frame?
options: To bring the entire length of the frame to 1500 bytes
To bring the length of the Data field to 46 bytes
To bring the length of the FCS field to 64 bytes
To bring the length of the SFD field to 1 byte
questions.explanation: Padding might be used in the Data and Pad field in order to bring it to the required minimum length of 46 bytes.

questions.question: What must you do in order to place VLAN 20 in the VLAN database on your local device?
options: Place the device in Client mode.
questions.explanation: To complete a VLAN configuration, you must exit config-vlan mode.

questions.question: What is the default VLAN in Cisco networking?
questions.explanation: VLAN 1 is the default VLAN in Cisco.

questions.question: How big is the VID field inside of the 802.1Q tag?
questions.explanation: The VID field is 12 bits in size.

questions.question: Which keyword is used to statically configure an EtherChannel?
questions.explanation: Use the on keyword when creating the EtherChannel in order to stati- cally configure the bundle. Note that this avoids both PAgP and LACP. questions.question: What command disables CDP globally on a device?
questions.explanation: You use the no cdp run command to globally disable CDP on a device. The cdp disable and cdp stop commands do not exist.

questions.question: What command ensures that CDP is running on a specific interface?
questions.explanation: You use the cdp enable command on an interface to run CDP on that specific interface.

questions.question: What is most often meant by the term “trimming” CDP?
options: Using version 1 of CDP to make it more efficient
Forwarding CDP messages to a different subnet by using a relay
Not running CDP on all interfaces
questions.explanation: Trimming CDP refers to disabling it selectively on certain interfaces.

questions.question: Which statement about CDP is correct?
options: CDP is disabled on all serial interfaces by default.
questions.explanation: CDP is a media- and protocol-independent Layer 2 protocol.

questions.question: On a Cisco device, what command would you use on an interface to ensure that the interface is sending LLDP frames?
questions.explanation: On an interface on a Cisco device, you can use the lldp transmit command to ensure that the interface sends LLDP frames. questions.question: What version of STP on a Cisco device implements classic IEEE 802.1D?
questions.explanation: PVST+ uses classic Spanning Tree Protocol on a Cisco device.

questions.question: What Spanning Tree Protocol feature is recommended for ports that connect to workstations or servers?
questions.explanation: The optional PortFast feature for ports that connect to workstations or servers improves convergence time.

questions.question: What command allows a port to more quickly transition from blocking to forwarding?
questions.explanation: The spanning-tree portfast command is applied in interface configuration mode and enables PortFast.

questions.question: What two new port roles were introduced with Rapid PVST+?
questions.explanation: The alternate and backup ports are new roles that exist to speed up convergence after topology changes (including failures). questions.question: What traditional 802.1D STP port state does not exist in 802.1w RSTP?
questions.explanation: The listening state does not exist in RSTP.

questions.question: Which RSTP port role functions to provide a fast converging replacement for the current root port?
questions.explanation: The alternate port is a fast converging alternative to the current root port. Answer Key to Practice Exam 2

questions.question: You are examining the interfaces on a Cisco WLAN controller. You observe an interface named Students-VLAN. What is most likely the type of this interface?
questions.explanation: In typical Cisco WLAN deployments, the WLAN is mapped to a dynamic interface on the WLAN controller.

questions.question: What is the purpose of spanning tree?
options: Cisco supported feature that can be used per VLAN
Can be initiated on any routed interface
questions.explanation: [BxNx] What is the purpose of spanning tree? a) Prevents bridging loops b) Prevents routing loops c) Cisco supported feature that can be used per VLAN d) Can be initiated on any routed interface e) All of the above Answer a) Prevents bridging loops c) Cisco supported feature that can be used per VLAN Transparent bridging requires that only one way exists through the bridge circuit to prevent routing loops. Also Cisco switching supports spanning t

questions.question: You are in console line configuration mode. Which of the following commands would place you in enable mode?
questions.explanation: end is the correct answer. The end com- mand and the Ctrl+Z key sequence both move the user to en- able mode regardless of the current configuration submode. 49

questions.question: In which of the following modes of the CLI could you configure the duplex setting for interface Fast Ethernet 0/2? 50
questions.explanation: Interface configuration mode is the correct answer. The speed and the duplex settings for Fast Ethernet 0/2 can be configured in the interface configuration mode.

questions.question: A Layer 2 switch configuration places all its physical ports into VLAN 2. The IP addressing plan shows that address 175.28.1.150 (with mask 255.255.255.0) is reserved for use by this new LAN switch and that 175.28.1.254 is already configured on the router connected to that same VLAN. The switch needs to support SSH connections into the switch 52 from any subnet in the network. Which of the following com- mands are part of the required configuration in this case? (Choose two answers.)
options: The switch cannot support SSH because all its ports connect to VLAN 2, and the IP address must be configured on interface VLAN 1.
The ip address 175.28.1.150 255.255.255.0 com- mand in interface vlan 2 configuration mode.
The ip default-gateway 175.28.1.254 command in global configuration mode.
The ip address 172.16.2.250 255.255.255.0 command in interface vlan 1 configuration mode.
questions.explanation: B and C are the correct answers. To allow SSH or Telnet access, a switch must have a correct IP configu- ration. That includes the configuration of a correct IP address and mask on a VLAN interface. That VLAN interface must have a path out of the switch via ports assigned to that VLAN. In this case, with all ports assigned to VLAN 2, the switch must use in- terface VLAN 2 (using the interface vlan 2 configuration com- mand).

questions.question: Which of the following configuration commands defines the password that all users must use to reach enable mode?
questions.explanation: A is the correct answer.

Vlan1 is up, line protocol is up Hardware is EtherSVI, address is 0023.e21b.4cc0 (bia 0023.e21b.4cc0) Internet address is 192.168.1.101/24 MTU 1500 bytes, BW 1000000 Kbit, DLY 10 usec, reliability 255/255, txload 1/255, rxload 1/255
questions.explanation: C is the correct answer.

options: configure terminal interface vlan 5 no shutdown
configure terminal interface vlan 5 ip address dhcp no shutdown
configure terminal native vlan dhcp ip address dhcp 59
